author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, Luke xii. 56, 58, ‘Ye know how to interpret the face of the Earth and the Heaven,’ etc.; and of the words, ‘for as thou art going with thine adversary before the magistrate, on the way give diligence to be quit of him,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke xii. 56, 58, ‘Ye know how to interpret the face of the Earth and the Heaven,’ etc.; and of the words, ‘for as thou art going with thine adversary before the magistrate, on the way give diligence to be quit of him,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have heard the Gospel, and in it the Lord reproving those who knew how to discern the face of the sky, and know not how to discover the time of faith, the kingdom of heaven which is at hand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now this He said to the Jews; but His words reach even unto us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now the Lord Jesus Christ Himself began the preaching of His Gospel in this way; “Repent ye, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In like manner too John the Baptist and His forerunner began thus; “Repent ye, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And now the Lord rebuketh those who would not repent, when “the kingdom of heaven was at hand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The kingdom of heaven,” as He saith Himself, “will not come with observation.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And again He saith, “The kingdom of heaven is within you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let every one then wisely receive the admonitions of the Master, that he may not lose the season of the mercy of the Saviour, which is now being dealt out, as long as the human race is spared.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to this end is man spared, that he may be converted, and that he may not be to be condemned.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God only knoweth when the end of the world shall come: nevertheless now is the time of faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whether the end of the world shall find any of us here, I know not; and perhaps it will not find us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our time is very near to each one of us, seeing we are mortal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We walk in the midst of chances.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we were made of glass, we should have to fear chances less than we have.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is more fragile than a vessel of glass?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet it is kept, and lasts for ages.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For though the chances of a fall are feared for the vessel of glass, yet there is no fear of fever or old age for it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We then are more fragile and more infirm; because all the chances which are incessant in human things, we doubtless through our frailness are in daily dread of; and if these chances come not, yet time goes on; a man avoids this stroke, can he avoid his end? he avoids accidents which happen from without, can that which is born within be driven away?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, now the entrails engender worms, now some other disease attacks on a sudden; lastly, let a man be spared ever so long, at last when old age comes, there is no way of putting off that.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore let us give ear to the Lord, let us do within ourselves what He hath enjoined.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us see who that adversary is, of whom He hath put us in fear, saying, “If thou goest with thine adversary to the magistrate, give diligence in the way to be delivered from him; lest haply he deliver thee to the magistrate, and the magistrate to the officer, and thou be cast into prison, from whence thou shalt not come out, till thou payest the very last farthing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is this “adversary”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the devil; we have been delivered from him already.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What a price was given for us that we might be redeemed from him!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of which the Apostle says, speaking of this our redemption, “Who hath delivered us from the power of darkness, and hath translated us into the kingdom of the Son of his love.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have been redeemed, we have renounced the devil; how shall we “give diligence to be delivered from him,” that he make us not, as sinners, his captives again?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this is not the “adversary” of whom the Lord gives us warning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in another place another Evangelist has so expressed it, that if we join both expressions together, and compare both expressions of the two Evangelists with each other, we shall soon understand who this adversary is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For see, what did Luke say here?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “When thou goest with thine adversary to the magistrate, give diligence in the way to be delivered from him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the other Evangelist has expressed this same thing thus: “Agree with thine adversary quickly, whiles thou art in the way with him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All the rest is alike: “Lest haply the adversary deliver thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and thou be cast into prison.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Both Evangelists have explained this alike.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One said, “Give diligence in the way to be delivered from him;” the other said, “Agree with him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou wilt not be able to “be delivered from him,” unless thou “agree with him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou “be delivered from him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Agree with him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what? is it the devil with whom the Christian ought to “agree”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then seek out this “adversary,” with whom we ought to “agree, lest he deliver us to the judge, and the judge to the officer;” let us seek him out, “and agree with him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou sin, the word of God is thine adversary.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For example, it is a delight to thee perchance to be drunken; it says to thee, “Do it not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a delight to thee to frequent the spectacles, and such triflings; it says to thee, “Do it not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a delight to thee to commit adultery; the word of God saith to thee, “Do it not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In what sins soever thou wouldest do thine own will, it saith to thee, “Do it not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is the adversary of thy will, till it become the author of thy salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O how goodly, how useful an “adversary”!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It does not seek our will, but our advantage.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is our “adversary,” as long as we are our own adversaries.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As long as thou art thine own enemy, thou hast the word of God thine enemy; be thine own friend, and thou art in agreement with it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou shalt do no murder;” give ear, and thou hast “agreed” with it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou shalt not steal;” give ear, and thou hast “agreed” with it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou shalt not commit adultery;” give ear, and thou hast “agreed” with it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou shalt not give false witness;” give ear, and thou hast “agreed” with it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s wife;” give ear, and thou hast agreed with it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou shalt not covet thy neighbour’s goods;” give ear, and thou hast “agreed” with it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In all these things thou hast agreed with this “thine adversary,” and what hast thou lost to thyself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not only hast thou lost nothing; but thou hast even found thyself, who hadst been lost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The way,” is this life; if we shall “agree with the adversary,” if we shall come to terms with him; when “the way” is ended, we shall not fear the “judge, the officer, the prison.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When is “the way” ended?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not ended at the same hour to all.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Each several man hath his hour when he shall end his “way.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This life is called “the way;” when thou hast ended this life, thou hast ended “the way.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We are going on, and the very living is advancing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Unless peradventure ye imagine that time advances, and we stand still!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It cannot be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As time advances, we too advance; and years do not come to us, but rather go away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Greatly are men mistaken when they say, “This boy has little good sense yet, but years will come on him, and he will be wise.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider what thou sayest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Will come on him,” thou hast said; “I will show that they go away,” whereas thou sayest, “they come on.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And hear how easily I prove it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us suppose that we have known the number of his years from his birth; for instance (that we may wish him well) he has to live fourscore years, he is to arrive at old age.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Write down fourscore years.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One year he has lived; how many hast thou in the total? how many hast thou down?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He has lived ten; seventy remain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He has lived twenty; sixty remain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet surely, it will be said, they did come; what can this mean?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our years come that they may depart; they come, I say that they may go.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they do not come, that they may abide with us, but as they pass through us, they wear us out, and make us less and less strong.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such is “the way” into which we have come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then have we to do with that “adversary,” that is, with the word of God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Agree with him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou knowest not when “the way” may be ended.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When “the way” is ended, there remain “the judge,” and “the officer,” and “the prison.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if thou maintain a good will to “thine adversary,” and “agree with him;” instead of a “judge,” shalt thou find a father, instead of a cruel “officer,” an Angel taking thee away into Abraham’s bosom, instead of a “prison,” paradise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How rapidly hast thou changed all things “in the way,” because thou hast “agreed with thine adversary”!
